steps.title: Connect data to the claim
steps.content: The researchers conclude that CEOs became increasingly interested in connecting with more departments. Data supporting that conclusion must show a rising number of department leaders reporting directly to CEOs across the studied periods.

steps.title: Reject nonrising patterns
steps.content: A says the averages did not fluctuate, which would not support growth. B describes a middle-period peak for managers, not a steady rise in department connections. C compares managers and department leaders within periods, but it does not show change over time. The answer is D.
